# Coding Plan for openclaw/openclaw#82014: Google Chat message-tool replies do not edit typing placeholder

Source report: [records/openclaw-openclaw/items/82014.md](records/openclaw-openclaw/items/82014.md)

## Summary

Keep open: current main still has separate Google Chat paths for automatic reply delivery and message-tool delivery. The automatic path receives and edits or clears `typingMessageName`, while current-source `message(action=send)` reaches the Google Chat action handler and sends directly without consuming that placeholder.

## Plan

Fix the Google Chat bug where current-source `message(action=send)` replies leave the message-backed typing placeholder visible. Keep the model-facing message tool and configuration unchanged. Add a Google Chat-owned bridge between the current turn's `typingMessageName` and same-target message-tool sends so the first text send updates the placeholder where practical, and otherwise deletes/clears it without affecting unrelated targets. Do not broaden core channel policy or change Google Chat thread-routing semantics beyond what is required for the placeholder lifecycle. Coordinate with the open thread-routing work at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/74235 and the NO_REPLY cleanup work at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39891; avoid duplicating those changes unless necessary for this bug. Add focused regression coverage for text and, if touched, media/current-source sends. Add a changelog entry for the user-visible Google Chat fix if the repair lane owns changelog updates.

## Likely Files

- `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.ts`
- `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-reply-delivery.ts`
- `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts`
- `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
- `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts`
- `src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts`
- `src/channels/plugins/types.public.ts`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
